body_txt: Many people would love to have a muscular physique to show off, whether it be to impress peers, intimidate enemies, or attract amorous attention. However, some of these are too impatient or lazy to put in the necessary training, and want to take a shortcut. Others may not want to grow their muscles permanently, because they only need it as a temporary costume or disguise. In either case the solution is to get fake muscles for show, usually by stuffing a large amount of padding inside one's shirt, wearing a muscle suit, or even painting muscles on. While they might be able to pull off a Pec Flex , they should be careful not to attempt any task that actually requires great strength . Alternatively, this often shows up in costume design because Form-Fitting Wardrobe is never as flattering to even a well-built actor as they might like, due to compression tight fabrics can render muscle definition flat or with unusual bulges . So the solution ends up sculpting a fake musculature into the costume or padding sewn in to help sell the Heroic Build . How convincing the muscles are and how well it sells the actor will vary. Compare Fake Boobs and Fake Facial Hair . See also Muscles Are Meaningless and Fat Flex .
